title: Gavilea platyantha
intro: Gavilea platyantha, also known as the large-flowered Gavilea or large-flowered orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits. Research has identified several bioactive constituents in Gavilea platyantha, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Gavilea platyantha is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Gavilea platyantha is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This classification places Gavilea platyantha in the monotypic genus Gavilea. Gavilea platyantha is further classified as a member of the subtribe Gigasporinae. The genus Gavilea is part of the tribe Gastrodiae. Gavilea platyantha is native to New Zealand.
